commit.message:
Fix UI: auto-size, URL wrapping, resize handle, position persistence, toggle-on deploy

1. Auto-size panel: calculates actual content height with dynamic row heights,
   capped at 70% screen. Empty state fully visible without scrolling (min 120pt).
2. URL text wrapping: numberOfLines=0 with word wrap, row height expands to fit.
3. Resize handle: bottom-right corner grip, pan to resize width+height.
4. Position persistence: saves frame to NSUserDefaults on drag/resize end,
   restores on next launch.
5. Toggle-on deploy: re-enabling a dylib immediately copies cached dylib to
   Tweaks folder (only 1 restart needed instead of 2).
6. Dynamic panel width: resize changes currentPanelWidth, all elements adapt.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
perf(licm): allow guard checks to be hoisted out of loops

Remove CheckMaps, CheckSmi, CheckNumber, CheckHeapObject, and all other
guard/checked-arithmetic nodes from the LICM is_pure exclusion list.

Guard checks only deoptimize on failure — they have no state-mutating
side effects. Hoisting them to the loop preheader is safe because:
- If the guard passes at loop entry, it passes for all iterations
  (shapes/types don't change without stores, which are already blocked)
- If the guard fails, we deopt immediately (cheaper than looping first)

This is critical for property_access benchmarks: CheckMaps was blocking
LoadNamedGeneric from being hoisted, keeping 5 property loads inside the
loop body instead of the preheader.

commit.message:
fix(attributeset): fix use-after-free in batch attribute removal (#1829)

AttributeSet_RemoveIdx processed removal indices from lowest to highest
(ascending). The swap-with-last pattern copies the value at position l-1
into the slot being removed. When l-1 itself is a removal target, the
copied value (including any heap pointer) becomes dangling when that
higher position is freed in a later iteration.

Reverse the loop direction to process highest indices first. This ensures
the swap source (l-1) is always a position that has already been processed
or is not scheduled for removal, preventing use-after-free and double-free
of heap-owning attribute values (strings, arrays, maps).

Bug was introduced in commit 0ddddf039 ("Reduce attribute-set memory
consumption") which added batch removal support.

Add regression test that batch-removes two and three heap-owning string
attributes including the last slot, verifying surviving values remain
valid and no crash occurs during cleanup.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(ci): detect untracked files in git-commit-data.sh change detection

When a slice file is deleted and recreated, it becomes untracked.
git diff --quiet only sees tracked files, causing the commit step to
report 'No changes detected' and skip the push.

Now also checks for untracked files in tracked directories (by-crawler/,
translation-cache/, etc.) before deciding there are no changes.

commit.message:
feat(research): EXP-1561 meal response metabolic characterization

Add ISF-normalized excursion and supply×demand spectral power analysis
by carb range, extending the Phase 1-2 natural experiments work:

Key findings:
- ISF normalization re-ranks patients: f (ISF=21) → 5.0 correction-equiv
  vs b (ISF=95) → 0.77, revealing true metabolic cost
- Spectral power scales 21× from 10-19g to ≥50g (super-linear)
- ISF-norm excursion and spectral power are ORTHOGONAL (r=-0.039) —
  independent dimensions capturing glycemic impact vs AID activity
- Carbs predict spectral power better (r=0.433) than excursion (r=0.149)
- Handles mmol/L ISF auto-detection via _extract_isf_scalar()

New artifacts:
- EXP-1561 experiment in exp_clinical_1551.py
- Phase 3 report: natural-experiments-metabolic-report-2026-04-09.md
- fig9: three-panel carb range (raw vs ISF-norm vs spectral)
- fig10: per-patient ISF normalization effect
- fig11: cross-metric scatter correlations
